content:
There's a number of routes to set the ring tone.

via Thunderbird app settings

1a. Nagivate to settings > account > notifications 1b. Click new mail ringtone. Assign a tone

2a. Nagivate to settings > account > notifications 2b. Click "messages" under Notification Categories header 2c. Click "ring" and choose a tone

via Android settings

3a. Navigate to Android settings > apps 3b. Click Thunderbird app 3c. Click manage notifications 3d. Click "messages" under email account header 3e. Click "Ring" and assign a tone.

content:
What protocol are you using for the server connection, imap or pop3. Pop3 is the legacy protocol and has limited capability versus imap. It doesn't support folders and intended for use with a single client.

Imap only folder sync

For emails in a folder to automatically sync, you need to enable the sync setting on each folder. Inbox is on by default.

To check a folder (TB Android)

  1. Open the account list
  2. Tap an account
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list)
  4. Tap a folder name (Spam as example)
  5. Toggle "sync" on

The app downloads messages up to 32KiB in size (default value). If you notice large sized emails have not downloaded, You can increase the max size. Navigate to settings > account > fetching mail > fetch messages up to:

Pop3 is more complex as it was never designed with multi-client use in mind. If you confirm a pop3 setup, some suggestions can be offered.

content:
You haven't provided much information to go on.

Does the app report any error messages? Have you taken any steps to fault find the issue? Are you able to receive any messages when manually synchronised? Are you able to successfully send an email? Are you connecting by imap or pop3?

The first test is to confirm the app is connecting successfully to the gmail server. Navigate to settings > account > ...

  1. fetching mail > incoming server.
  2. sending mail > outgoing server.

Tap next on each screen and confirm the server connection succeeds.

Under settings > account > fetching mail Confirm folder poll frequency = 15 min

Check sync is enabled on inbox and other imap folders.

  1. Open the account list
  2. Tap account
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list)
  4. Tap folder name (e.g inbox)
  5. Check "enable sync" = on

Repeat for each folder as applicable.

content:
Are you using "push" in general terms to highlight that emails are not being reguarly synchronised (15 min polling), or do you specifically have an issue with the push service which delivers emails immediatly upon arrival on the server.

For general synchronisation issues, Have you checked the app is excluded from any Android battery optimisations and allowed it to run unrestricted in the background.

  1. Long press Thunderbirds launcher icon.
  2. Click battery usage.
  3. Enable background usage.

The location and name for these settings can vary by phone manufacturer and android version.

Push Service

Android requires the app's push service to run in the foreground and display a permanant notification.

If you close the app using the recent app list, the push service is terminated and won't receive new emails.

If you're using the push service with Android 13 or later, check you've granted the app "Alarm & reminders" permission.

See the guide linked below. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/configure-push-email-thunderbird-android

content:
The app was built to comply with the imap specification (RFC 3501).

The "Server Internal Date Message Attribute" is used to determine which messages are fetched. This will likely hold the "Arrival Time" when you imported the mbox file to the servers inbox.

RFC 3501 https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3501#section-2.3.3

2.3.3. Internal Date Message Attribute 

  The internal date and time of the message on the server.  This   is not the date and time in the [RFC-2822] header, but rather a   date and time which reflects when the message was received.  In   the case of messages delivered via [SMTP], this SHOULD be the   date and time of final delivery of the message as defined by   [SMTP].  In the case of messages delivered by the IMAP4rev1 COPY   command, this SHOULD be the internal date and time of the source   message.  In the case of messages delivered by the IMAP4rev1   APPEND command, this SHOULD be the date and time as specified in   the APPEND command description.  All other cases are   implementation defined.

content:
[The first question is, do you think this is really the root cause for my problem?]

It's one possible explanation but couldn't say for certain. I've never tried importing from an mbox file. A perplexity AI search advised the date applied is down to the behavior of the import tool.

When you import from an mbox file, most tools use IMAP APPEND to push messages into the new mailbox and either:

  • Pass the original “received”/header date as the APPEND date argument, in which case Internal Date will match the original server’s receive time.
  • Or omit a date argument, in which case the server sets Internal Date to “now”, i.e., the time of import

Did your import tool offer any settings or options ?

[The second is, why exactly would TB for PC work different from TB for Android]

That's comparing apples to oranges. TB desktop and TB Android (K-9) have different heritages and code bases. Your supposition is reasonable.

Q1) Are your missing emails large in size. Have you tried increasing the "fetch message up to 32KiB" setting to a larger size?

Q2) Can you try changing the setting "Sync messages from". Set to "The last 3 months" and see if any missing messages download.

content:
Hi Naturalp

You need to use the same release type to upgrade. Beta version > newer beta version. Stable version > newer stable version.

Stable versions can't upgrade betas and vice versa. The upgrades must come from the same store as your current install, Google Play, F-Droid or github.

  • Play store beta: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=net.thunderbird.android.beta&hl=en-US
  • F-Droid store beta: https://f-droid.org/packages/net.thunderbird.android.beta/
  • Github releases: https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/releases

Stable version

The standard version can be installed side-by-side with beta's, through a fresh install rather than an upgrade. On first launch, you have the option to import your settings from the beta version.

content:
Thanks for confirming Imap is used.

You advise automatic(none) is assigned to the draft folder under settings > account > folders. If 'Draft' is unavailable for selection in the drop down folder list, can you login to your providers webmail settings. There's two options you can check / try.

1. Some providers have a setting allowing you to enable and disable which folders are available via imap. If cock.li provides the option, you should enable for the draft folder. I've attached a screen shot from gmail as example.

2. If your webmail doesn't have a 'Draft' special folder, try creating a folder or label named 'draft' and enable it to show in imap.

Once completed, refresh the Thunderbird folder list ...

  1. Open Thunderbird for Android
  2. Open the account list
  3. tap your cock.li account
  4. tap 'manage folders' (bottom of list)
  5. tap 'refresh folders'

3. Navigate to settings > account > folders > draft folder and select the 'Draft' folder.

content:
There are a couple of ways to do the same task.

Via in app settings

Navigate to settings > account > notifications

  • Set 'new mail ringtone' to none, or
  • a. Tap 'messages' under the 'notification categories' section.
  • b. Toggle 'set as silent' or set the ringtone to 'none'

You can set also set quiet time hours so you don't get notifications at night Navigate to settings > general settings > notifications Enable quiet time and set the desired time window.

Via standard android notification settings.

  1. Long press the Thunderbird launcher icon
  2. Tap app info
  3. Tap manage notifications
  4. Tap 'messages' under your mail account name
  5. Either toggle 'set as silent' or set the ringtone to 'none'

content:
Synchronisation is the process that keeps Thunderbird for Android's mailbox view aligned with the server. It checks for mailbox changes, retrieves new emails and sends outbound ones.

The app runs this process (polling) typically every 15 minutes.

If you click the 'Sync all accounts' button, it will check for new emails on each account immediately rather that waiting till the next 15 minute interval is reached.

Hi Joe

The app is undergoing a UI redesign. The developers needed to remove the font settings while these changes are being made. Customisation options taylored to the new user interface will likely be included in a future update.

The developers provided the below response on github when font controls were removed.

"We’ve removed these settings as part of the ongoing UI redesign, since the previous way of changing font sizes no longer works with the new design system. We’re working on alternative options to bring back some customization in a future update."
